domains.description: L1 index of methodology domains. The retriever reads this first, picks 1-3 candidate domains, then reads only those domains' INDEX.xml.

domain.scope: AI agents and agentic systems - agent architectures, tool use, multi-agent orchestration, memory, planning, evaluation, and production-grade agent patterns.
Core AI / ML foundations - model selection, prompt engineering basics, embeddings, RAG fundamentals, LLM integration patterns, and the underlying primitives that AI-powered features are built from.
Software architecture - ADRs, API gateway patterns, service boundaries, reversibility tagging, observability design, and other software-architect decisions that shape long-lived systems.

Business analysis - requirements elicitation, process modeling, stakeholder management, KPI design, change-request impact analysis, BA core practice and modeling techniques bridging product strategy and delivery.

Backend engineering - APIs, services, databases, server-side patterns across Python/Node/Java/Rails/PHP/.NET, including auth, caching, queues, background work, contract-first design and enterprise framework patterns.
Claude Code authoring stack - agents, commands, hooks, skills, MCP servers, and project documentation conventions for building reliable agentic tooling on top of Claude.

Communication craft for solopreneurs and small agencies - stakeholder updates, executive demos, contractor and freelancer scripts, negotiation, storytelling, difficult conversations, and customer-facing testimonials.
Software engineering practice - coding craft, code quality, testing, devtools. The dev bucket covers anything a software developer does day-to-day.
Frontend and UI engineering - design systems, component libraries, accessibility, performance, state management, build tooling, and UI/UX implementation patterns for web and spatial interfaces.
HR and recruiting practice - full-cycle hiring, structured interview design, STAR method, candidate experience, employer branding, EVP, 30-60-90 onboarding plans, and retention compliance.
Infrastructure and platform engineering - DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, observability, server craft, cloud, networking, deploy and operate everything that runs the code in production.
Marketing practice - content, SEO, growth, PPC, SMM, CRO, GTM. Everything that drives demand and converts it into customers.

Applied ML / AI engineering practice - building, evaluating, deploying, and operating LLM and ML systems: RAG pipelines, agentic patterns, eval-driven development, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, model rollout and MLOps for production AI features.
Product + project management - planning, prioritization, stakeholder management, agile/traditional ceremonies, ops, KPIs and roadmap delivery.
Product management discipline - discovery, opportunity sizing, product strategy, launch planning, post-launch review, product operations and account health.
Research practice - user research, market research, qualitative coding protocols, AI-assisted persona building, interview analysis and research synthesis.
Spec-driven development - constitution, roadmap, spec, design, test plan, implementation plan, ADR consequence binding, AI-assisted spec writing, and the full SDD lifecycle from backlog to done.

Wiring AI agents into the SDLC - lang/lint/test/tracker/kb/task/mr/inc/sec/gov methodologies that connect agents to the development pipeline floor: PR review, kb maintenance, incident triage, governance.
Application and supply-chain security - CVE triage, dependency audit cadence, and other repeatable security artefacts for solo and small-team development.
UX design and research practice - UI design, UX research, user research, accessibility specialist work, design ops, usability and conversion design.
